title: San Francisco Sourdough Boule

seo.title: San Francisco Sourdough Boule Recipe: Bake at Home!
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of baking a classic San Francisco Sourdough Boule at home. Get our authentic recipe for a crusty exterior and chewy, tangy interior. Perfect for beginners!
longTailKeywords: how to make San Francisco Sourdough Boule
easy San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
authentic San Francisco sourdough boule recipe
San Francisco sourdough boule step by step
best San Francisco sourdough boule at home
San Francisco sourdough boule starter recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: homemade San Francisco sourdough boule recipe

sourdough boule recipe San Francisco style
seo.h1: Authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule Recipe
h2Headings: What is a San Francisco Sourdough Boule?
Tips for the Perfect Crust and Crumb
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked San Francisco Sourdough Boule with a golden crust and open crumb on a wooden cutting board.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the iconic tang and chew of a homemade San Francisco Sourdough Boule. Perfect for sandwiches or alongside your favorite meal!

description: This iconic bread captures the essence of San Francisco with its characteristic tangy flavor and chewy, open crumb. Perfect for slicing thin for sandwiches or serving with a simple smear of butter, this homemade boule brings the bakery experience right to your kitchen. Its crisp, golden crust gives way to a soft, airy interior, making every bite a delight.

instructions.title: Mix the Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the active sourdough starter and warm water, stirring until the starter is mostly dissolved. Add the bread flour and whole wheat flour, mixing with a wooden spoon or your hands until no dry spots remain. Cover the bowl with a damp cloth or plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes (autolyse).

instructions.title: Incorporate Salt and Bulk Fermentation
instructions.text: Sprinkle the salt over the dough. Wet your hands and gently incorporate the salt by squeezing and folding the dough for a few minutes. Cover and let the dough rest. Over the next 3-4 hours, perform 4-5 sets of stretch and folds every 30-45 minutes. To do a stretch and fold, wet your hand, grab a portion of the dough, stretch it upwards, and fold it over onto itself. Rotate the bowl and repeat until you have gone all the way around. The dough should become smoother and more elastic.

instructions.title: Pre-shape and Rest
instructions.text: Gently turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Using a bench scraper, gently shape the dough into a loose round. Avoid deflating the dough too much. Cover it with the bowl or a damp cloth and let it rest for 20-30 minutes. This allows the gluten to relax, making the final shaping easier.

instructions.title: Final Shape and Cold Proof
instructions.text: Lightly flour your work surface again. Gently shape the dough into a tight boule, creating tension on the surface. Transfer the shaped dough, seam-side up, into a floured banneton or a bowl lined with a floured kitchen towel. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 12 hours, or up to 24 hours. Cold proofing develops flavor and makes the dough easier to handle.

instructions.title: Preheat Oven and Dutch Oven
instructions.text: About an hour before baking, place your Dutch oven with its lid into your oven and preheat to 475F (245C). Ensure the Dutch oven is fully heated through before adding the dough, as this creates the necessary steam for a great crust.

instructions.title: Score and Bake
instructions.text: Carefully remove the hot Dutch oven from the oven. Gently invert the cold dough from the banneton into the hot Dutch oven. Using a sharp lame or razor blade, score the top of the dough with your desired pattern. Place the lid back on the Dutch oven and bake for 20 minutes. After 20 minutes, remove the lid and continue to bake for another 20-25 minutes, or until the crust is deeply golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 200-210F (93-99C).

instructions.title: Cool Completely
instructions.text: Carefully remove the boule from the Dutch oven and transfer it to a wire rack to cool completely for at least 1-2 hours before slicing. Slicing too early will result in a gummy texture. Enjoy your homemade San Francisco Sourdough Boule!

tips: For the best open crumb, ensure your starter is very active and bubbly before mixing. It should float in water.
Do not skip the cold proofing step. It significantly enhances the flavor and develops that signature sourdough tang.
Always let your sourdough cool completely on a wire rack before slicing. This allows the internal moisture to redistribute, preventing a gummy texture.
article: San Francisco Sourdough Boule is a beloved American Baking dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
